incorrects.description: Student did not explore all four scale options on first attempt

steps.text: Try clicking each scale button. You need to see all four.

incorrects.description: Student did not explore all four scale options on second attempt

params.description: All four scale buttons (1, 2, 5, 10) pulse briefly.

steps.text: Click each of the four scale buttons. When you check all of them, you will see which ones fit this data.

incorrects.description: Student did not explore all four scale options after three attempts. System models the exploration process.

params.description: Scale 1 preview displays briefly with all bars visible, 24 tick marks on axis.
Scale 2 preview displays briefly with all bars visible.
Scale 5 preview displays briefly with all bars visible.
Scale 10 preview displays briefly with all bars visible.

steps.text: I clicked through all four scale buttons. Scale of 1 shows 24 tick marks. Scale of 2 shows fewer. Scales of 5 and 10 show even fewer. All four scales fit because the biggest number, 23, fits on each axis. When data is small, you have more scale choices.

incorrects.description: Student selected Scale of 2 instead of Scale of 1

params.description: Graph updates to Scale of 2. All bars visible.

steps.text: That works, but the prompt asked for Scale of 1. Try that one.

incorrects.description: Student selected Scale of 5 instead of Scale of 1

params.description: Graph updates to Scale of 5. All bars visible.

steps.text: That works, but the prompt asked for Scale of 1. Try that one.

incorrects.description: Student selected Scale of 10 instead of Scale of 1

params.description: Graph updates to Scale of 10. All bars visible.

steps.text: That works, but the prompt asked for Scale of 1. Try that one.

incorrects.description: Student did not select Scale of 1 after multiple attempts. System models the selection.

params.description: Graph updates to Scale of 1. All four bars displayed. Axis shows 24 tick marks from 0 to 23.

steps.text: I clicked Scale of 1. Look at the axis now. There are 24 tick marks, one for every number from 0 to 23. All the bars land exactly on a line, but that is a lot of lines on the side. Scale of 1 is exact, but when the biggest number is above 10 or so, the axis gets crowded.
